decision_scenario: Lo! Foods wants you on a subscription. Given your actual eating and shopping behaviour — how often you cook, how often you order in, how predictable your weekly routine is — which subscription format (if any) would you genuinely maintain for 3+ months? What's the single biggest reason health food subscriptions fail for people like you, and what would Lo! Foods need to do differently?

rounds.stimulus: Lo! Foods has launched four subscription options. Option A: Daily fresh keto meal delivery via Swiggy (5 days/week, ₹2,500/month — saves 15% vs ordering individually). Option B: Weekly Protein Chef bread + snack kit delivered to your door (4 items/week curated by a nutritionist, ₹1,500/month). Option C: Monthly 'Lo! Essentials' box — 8 packaged products (bread, cookies, biscuits, protein powder) at ₹999/month, 20% off MRP. Option D: Flexible subscription — choose your own products and frequency, minimum ₹500/month, 12% off. Which format feels most aligned with how you actually eat and shop?

I'd treat it less like a 'subscription' and more like a loyalty discount on products I'm already buying anyway.
